$doc: The loop's driver — machine-readable on purpose: readiness, batches, cap and
thrash breaches, phase drain, and completion are computed by schedule.mjs,
never by eye. Append-mostly: never delete a ticket; mark it done or decomposed.
status: todo | in-progress | done | blocked | decomposed ('ready' is computed).
phase ties the ticket to its spec phase — the scheduler reports drain from it;
decomposed children inherit the parent's phase.
files is a CONTRACT and must be NON-EMPTY: the verifier diffs the branch from
its recorded fork point (baseSha) and fails undeclared touches (package.json +
lockfiles allowlisted for every ticket); the scheduler rejects empty files.
attempts is the durable diagnosis log — one entry per failed attempt; failed is
an ARRAY of check names so the scheduler detects thrash mechanically.
evidence is a POINTER into .ailoop/evidence/ — never inline captured output;
this file is re-read every loop turn.
caps.chunk counts worker DISPATCHES per invocation (batch members and retries

tickets.title: <one-line title>
tickets.origin: spec §X.Y | decomposed from T0NN | repair: gate red after <ids>
tickets.context: Everything a cold subagent needs and nothing it doesn't: which spec sections govern this (quote the binding constraints); which locked decisions apply (cite oracle.md); what already exists (which prior tickets built what it depends on); the precise deliverable — build only this.
tickets.acceptance:
The ticket-local oracle — checkable, not vibes; ON TOP OF the baseline gate in oracle.md. Prefer input→output contrast checks over artifact-existence checks (existence is the most gameable). Add tests for new behavior (skip only for pure scaffold/config).
- `<command>` exits 0
- <behavioral check with expected result>

attempts.fixNote: the targeted instruction given on re-dispatch
